code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test16-DEC-2016Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and mild hyperchloremia. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria and glycosuria, with low specific gravity, but no hematuria or leukocyturia. These findings are consistent with possible diabetic nephropathy or another form of renal impairment. Further nephrological evaluation and assessment for diabetes are recommended.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
